items.name: agents365-ai.drawio-skill.drawio-skill
items.description: Generate draw.io diagrams with rich shape support and local CLI export workflows for architecture, UML, ERD, sequence, and flow diagrams.

items.name: anthropics.skills.frontend-design
items.description: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality that avoid generic AI aesthetics. Use when building web components, pages, artifacts, posters, or applications.

items.name: dietrichgebert.ponytail.ponytail
items.description: Enforces a minimal, YAGNI-first coding style with lite/full/ultra intensity modes.

items.name: dietrichgebert.ponytail.ponytail-audit
items.description: Audits an entire repository for over-engineering and ranks simplification opportunities.

items.name: dietrichgebert.ponytail.ponytail-debt
items.description: Collects ponytail-marked shortcuts into a debt ledger with explicit upgrade triggers.

items.name: dietrichgebert.ponytail.ponytail-help
items.description: Displays a quick-reference card for Ponytail modes, skills, and commands.

items.name: dietrichgebert.ponytail.ponytail-review
items.description: Reviews diffs for over-engineering and reports what to delete or simplify.

items.name: github.awesome-copilot.prompt-builder
items.description: Expert prompt engineering and validation workflow for creating and improving high-quality prompts.

items.name: kunchenguid.chrome-devtools-axi.chrome-devtools-axi
items.description: Control a Chrome browser session through the chrome-devtools-axi CLI - navigate, snapshot, click, fill forms, run JavaScript, inspect console and network, take screenshots, audit performance. Prefer this over other browser automation tools.

items.name: kunchenguid.lavish-axi.lavish
items.description: Turn complex or visual agent responses into rich, reviewable HTML artifacts the user can annotate and send feedback on, using the lavish-axi CLI. Use when about to give a plan, comparison, diagram, table, code diff, report, or anything easier to grasp visually than as prose.

items.name: local.agent-memory-workflow
items.description: Use when writing, searching, or maintaining entries in the .ai/memory/ vault.

items.name: local.awesome-ai-usage
items.description: Use when managing the awesome-ai skills-registry in any onboarded project — installing, uninstalling, updating, listing, searching, or syncing skills and instructions. Covers the full CLI, agent paths, lock file, profiles, and team sharing.

items.name: local.baseline-agents
items.description: Behavioral guidelines to reduce common LLM coding mistakes. A baseline AGENTS.md for any project.

items.name: local.context-sync
items.description: Use when you need to update project context files to reflect the current state of the codebase, or after making significant changes to the project structure, conventions, or architecture.

items.name: local.example-skill
items.description: An example skill demonstrating the manifest format and skill structure.

items.name: mattpocock.skills.grill-with-docs
items.description: Grilling session that challenges your plan against the existing domain model, sharpens terminology, and updates CONTEXT.md and ADRs inline.

items.name: obra.superpowers.brainstorming
items.description: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation.

items.name: obra.superpowers.dispatching-parallel-agents
items.description: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies

items.name: obra.superpowers.executing-plans
items.description: Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints

items.name: obra.superpowers.finishing-a-development-branch
items.description: Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work - guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup

items.name: obra.superpowers.receiving-code-review
items.description: Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable - requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation

items.name: obra.superpowers.requesting-code-review
items.description: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements

items.name: obra.superpowers.subagent-driven-development
items.description: Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session

items.name: obra.superpowers.systematic-debugging
items.description: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes

items.name: obra.superpowers.test-driven-development
items.description: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code

items.name: obra.superpowers.using-git-worktrees
items.description: Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from current workspace or before executing implementation plans - creates isolated git worktrees with smart directory selection and safety verification

items.name: obra.superpowers.using-superpowers
items.description: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions

items.name: obra.superpowers.verification-before-completion
items.description: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always

items.name: obra.superpowers.writing-plans
items.description: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code

items.name: obra.superpowers.writing-skills
items.description: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment

items.name: shadcn.improve.improve
items.description: Senior advisor skill that audits codebases and writes self-contained implementation plans for other agents to execute.

items.name: local.agent-memory
items.description: Procedural commands forcing the agent to search .ai/memory/ before tasks and propose entries after tasks.

items.name: local.opensrc-source-context
items.description: Optional guidance for using opensrc to fetch dependency source code when deeper implementation context is needed.

items.name: local.taste-setup
items.description: One-time setup prompt asking whether to enable the Taste Developer skill for adaptive preference learning.
